contextBlurb: Virginia has 3 relevant bills tracked in 2025–2026 — 1 on data-center policy and 2 on AI regulation. 1 has been enacted into law. 2 advance stricter regulation.

legislation.title: Consumer Data Protection Act; social media platforms & model operators, interoperability interfaces.
legislation.summary: This Virginia bill requires social media platforms and AI system operators to build open interfaces so users can move their personal connection data and AI-related data to other services. It places significant technical and compliance obligations on covered platforms, taking effect in 2027.

legislation.title: Artificial intelligence; framework for person/entity acting as an independent verification org.
legislation.summary: This bill directs a Virginia legislative commission to study whether the state should create a framework for independent organizations that verify AI systems meet safety standards. The commission must report its findings and recommendations by November 2026, but no regulations are enacted yet.

legislation.title: Law-enforcement officers; restrictions on wearing of facial coverings, exceptions, penalty.
legislation.summary: This bill regulates when law enforcement officers can wear physical face coverings (like masks) while on duty, with limited exceptions. It has no connection to AI policy, facial recognition technology, or data-center regulation.

legislation.title: Virginia All-Payer Claims Database; disclosure of information, limitation.
legislation.summary: This bill limits what health insurance payment data can be shared from Virginia's statewide medical claims database. It is not related to AI or data-center policy.

legislation.title: Retail Sales and Use Tax; exemption for data centers, delayed effective date.
legislation.summary: This bill ties Virginia's existing data center sales tax exemption to new clean energy requirements, including phased bans on carbon-emitting generators and mandates to source energy from clean resources. Data centers that don't meet these environmental and efficiency standards will lose their tax break starting in 2027.

legislation.title: Court records; aggregated case data, request by attorney for the Commonwealth exempted.
legislation.summary: This bill allows Commonwealth attorneys to request court records with identifying information like names and partial Social Security numbers for official duties. It is not related to AI or data-center policy.

legislation.title: Mental health & substance abuse disorders; network adequacy standards, comparative analyses, report.
legislation.summary: This bill updates Virginia's mental health and substance abuse insurance coverage rules, including network adequacy standards and federal parity law compliance requirements. It has no connection to AI policy or data-center regulation.

legislation.title: Mental health & substance abuse disorders; network adequacy standards, comparative analyses, report.
legislation.summary: This bill sets network adequacy standards for mental health and substance abuse treatment coverage under health insurance plans in Virginia. It is not related to AI or data-center policy.

legislation.title: Adults charged with criminal offenses punishable by incarceration; Va. longitudinal Data System.
legislation.summary: This bill allows Virginia's Criminal Sentencing Commission to share data about adults charged with criminal offenses with a statewide education data system, while keeping personal information confidential. It is primarily a data-sharing and record-keeping measure, not an AI or data-center policy bill.

legislation.title: Adults charged with criminal offenses punishable by incarceration; Va. longitudinal Data System.
legislation.summary: This bill allows a Virginia criminal sentencing body to share its collected data on adults charged with crimes into a statewide longitudinal data system, while keeping personal information confidential. It is primarily a data-sharing and record-keeping measure, not an AI or data-center policy bill.

legislation.title: Prescription Drug Affordability Board; established.
legislation.summary: This bill establishes a Prescription Drug Affordability Advisory Panel to study and recommend policies on drug pricing, and caps certain drug prices at federally set maximum fair prices. It has no relevance to AI regulation or data-center policy.

legislation.title: Prescription Drug Affordability Board; established.
legislation.summary: This bill creates a board to study and limit prescription drug prices in Virginia, requiring price transparency from drug manufacturers and pharmacy benefit managers. It has no connection to AI or data-center policy.
